Dear Sir:
My name is Dr. Robert F. Burns, and I am a candidate for
re election to the Lemon crove City Council on June 2, 1992. I own
a pro,)erty al 7572 North Ave. , in Lenon Grove shich is approx-
imately 100'by 140' and located on the North side of the street.
North Ave. is in a Heavy cornmercial zone. and is 60' rside and classed
as a commercial collector street. 
The business on the property is
a Veterinary Hospital vith a large parking 1ot. I started the bus-
iness in 1955 and operated it as a sole proprietor until Dec. of
1990 when I sold the business to a Dr. E.P. Quines. I sold all of my
equipment, records, supplies, etc. to Dr. ouines and have not part-
icipated in the Veterinary practise since that time. I am leasing
him the property ( building and land) now in the second year of a
five year lease with an option to renew for an additional five years.
He also has the riEht of first refusal should there be any occaision
to consider sale of the properly, and of course the existing lease
would have to be honored should a sale take place. In my present
situation, I have no desire or inclination to sell the property, since
our financialFituation is secure and it is rel-egated to being a part
of our estate.
The property is within the Lemon Grove Redevelopment area but
is not considered for any kind of project, and the nature of the par-
ce1 is such that it is not likely to be. However, across the street
to lhe South, almost the entire btock ( 10 acres) is currently des-
ignated as a project area to become a Hone Depot new location' 
Since
ny property is actually only 60 feet f rorn the project area' T have
abstained from discussion and <iecisions regarding the project ' I vas
in on the discussions but that I should neither
time so as not to influence the decisions'


Our City Attourney has informed ne that because of the long
term lease of my property, and the fact that I have sold the business
and am not participating in it othervise. that it may be possible for
me to be involved vith the project as a Councilman, and not be in a
situation of conflict of interest. He suggested that I r4rrite to you for
an opinion and act accordingly. As I see it, there may well be some
long term benefits ol. harm come to my property va1ue, with the pre-
sence of the Home Depot establishment across the street. The increased
traff i.c and the higher visibility 
to many more people could cert-
ainly have an effect on the Veterinary Hospital business, probably
in both negative and positive ways. Other than the property value,
in being in close proximity to the large commercial enterprise, I do
not see that I would be in great conflict of interest.
since the project is going to be relocating, with some degree
of pain, many of my friends and o1d clients in the area. I have not
been a1I thatftfiappy ffith the ,, abstain,, posture but witl take your
opinion as what I should do. I do not wish to shirk my duty as a Coun-
cilman either so wiLl a{ait your decision.
verytruryyours ?n;r"
Dr. Robert F. Burns
Councilman- City of Lemon Grove

sections.question_synthetic: Can Dr. Burns, a Lemon Grove City Councilman and candidate for re-election, participate in decisions regarding a Home Depot redevelopment project located across the street from his leased veterinary hospital property, or does he have a conflict of interest that requires abstention?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The letter contains the questioner's detailed background but no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The inquiry is embedded within the narrative text of the letter. Document is from 1992 and concerns conflict of interest under FPPC regulations.

embedding.summary: Dr. Robert F. Burns, a Lemon Grove City Councilman, seeks FPPC guidance on whether he can participate in city decisions regarding a Home Depot redevelopment project located approximately 60 feet from his leased commercial property (veterinary hospital), given potential effects on property value and business operations.
